fundamentals.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Spotify Technology S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides audio streaming subscription services worldwide. It operates in two segments, Premium and Ad-Supported. The Premium segment offers online and offline streaming access to its catalog of music and podcasts, including video, lossless music, and audiobooks in select markets through subscription offerings primarily sold directly to end users and partners. The Ad-Supported segment provides limited on-demand online access to its catalog of music and online and offline access to its catalog of podcasts on computers, tablets, mobile devices, and other smart devices. The company also offers sales, distribution and marketing, contract research and development, and customer and other support services. Spotify Technology S.A. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

judgment.summary: Strong growth and profitability metrics are offset by high leverage and a premium valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Revenue growth 8.2% YoY, with analyst estimates for current Q at 769.9% and current FY at 22.4%.
Earnings growth 222.4% YoY, with quarterly earnings growth at 220.4%.
Operating margin 15.8% and profit margin 15.4% indicate healthy profitability.
Return on equity 38.0% and return on assets 11.6% show efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow of $707.75M TTM and a current ratio of 2.06 suggest good liquidity.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 5.94 indicates high financial leverage.
Forward P/E of 27.95 and PEG ratio of 1.79 suggest a premium valuation relative to growth.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 6.06 is also on the higher side.
Beta of 1.55 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Spotify's stock has seen a notable increase of 15.6% since its last earnings report, driven partly by a new AI remix collaboration with Universal Music Group. Despite this positive momentum and strong analyst consensus for a 'buy' rating, some reports suggest the stock may still be overvalued. The company's growth in revenue and earnings has been substantial, with significant analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year.
